text.tspan: Pimato Sandad is a neography (devised writing system) designed by Ander Jiloh in 2016. Pimato means 'letters' and sandad means 'native' in
the Kadazandusun language. Pimato Sandad can be used to write not only Kadazandusun, but also any Momogun dialect (Dusunic, Murutic, or
Paitanic). In fact, any indigenous Bornean language (Lun Bawang, Kayan, Iban, Kelabit, Kenyah, etc.) can also be written using Pimato Sandad.

tspan.tspan: It does not work like the Latin writing system (ABCs) because it is not an alphabet. Pimato Sandad is an
syllables are written as one composite glyph. The look of each glyph is inspired by shoots sprouting out of the ground, as well as plant tendrils,
all of which also appear in indigenous Bornean art.
(designed by King Sejong of Joseon), and
forms a growing list of devised writing systems which were deliberately formulated and invented
by speakers of the languages they seek to write.
